chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْقَوْلِ عِنْدَ الْإِصْبَاحِ وَ الْإِمْسَاءِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 48 – The words (to be spoken) during the morning and the evening

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/286)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/286)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ التَّسْبِيحِ فَقَالَ مَا عَلِمْتُ شَيْئاً مُوَظَّفاً غَيْرَ تَسْبِيحِ فَاطِمَةَ ( عليها السلام ) وَ عَشْرَ مَرَّاتٍ بَعْدَ الْفَجْرِ تَقُولُ.
لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَ لَهُ الْحَمْدُ يُحْيِي وَ يُمِيتُ وَ هُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْ‏ءٍ قَدِيرٌ

en.hubeali: From him, from Ibn Mahboub, from Al A’ala Bin Razeyn, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said,

‘I asked Abu Ja’farasws about the Glorification (Tasbeeh). So heasws said: ‘Iasws do not know of anything other than the Tasbeeh of Fatimaasws; and ten times after Al-Fajr (Salat) you should be saying,

‘There is no god except Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, Alone, there being no associates for Him<sup>azwj</sup>. For Him<sup>azwj</sup> is the Kingdom, and for Him<sup>azwj</sup> is the Praise; and He<sup>azwj</sup> Curses to live and He<sup>azwj</sup> Causes to die, and He<sup>azwj</sup> is Able upon everything’.
And then you can Glorify whatever you so desire to, voluntarily’.
en.sarwar: 34. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from ibn Mahbub from al- ‘Ala’ ibn Razin from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “Once I asked abu Ja’far (a.s.) about Tasbih (words to glorify Allah). The Imam said, ‘I do not know of anything other than Tasbih of Fatimah (a.s.) and ten times after the morning prayer, ‘No one deserves to be worshipped except Allah, alone Who has no partner, to Him belongs all praise, He gives life, causes death. He has power over all things,’ and then say Tasbih voluntarily as much as you wish.’” (! Tasbih of Fatimah (a.s.) consists of saying, ‘Allah is greater than can be described,’ thirty-four times ‘all praise belongs to Allah,’ thirty-three times and ‘Allah is free of defects,’ thirty-three times.)
